prompt:
Assign GRADE certainty ratings to each evidence item below.
Ratings: HIGH (systematic review of RCTs, low heterogeneity), MODERATE (RCT with some concerns, or consistent observational), LOW (observational studies, high heterogeneity, indirect evidence), VERY_LOW (case reports, expert opinion, serious limitations).

For each item, output ONLY the number and rating, one per line:
1. HIGH
2. MODERATE
...

EVIDENCE:
1. [SILVER] Source: Enhancement of resistance against damp heat aging through co | Statement: PEVA films with 18, 24, 33, and 40% VA content were fabricated, cured at 150 degrees C under vacuum, and subjected to DH aging at 85 degrees C / 85% R
2. [SILVER] Source: Enhancement of resistance against damp heat aging through co | Statement: Tensile strength, thermal stability, and crosslinking degree were greater for PEVA with lower VA content (18% and 24%) and diminished for higher VA co
3. [SILVER] Source: Enhancement of resistance against damp heat aging through co | Statement: PEVA with 18% VA was discouraged as encapsulant due to inadequate transmittance and high stiffness after 2000 hours of DH aging.
4. [SILVER] Source: Enhancement of resistance against damp heat aging through co | Statement: PEVAs with 33% and 40% VA degraded significantly after 2000 hours DH aging with significant loss in transmittance and mechanical integrity.
5. [SILVER] Source: Enhancement of resistance against damp heat aging through co | Statement: Results encourage the PV industry to reduce VA content in PEVA to 24% for better encapsulation response against damp heat aging.
